_comment: Routing table for Hestia's detect-and-route orchestration. Hestia DETECTS config drift but does not repair it — repair belongs to the plugin that owns the artifact. Claude Code exposes no programmatic cross-plugin invocation API (verified against plugins-reference), so a 'handoff' is: name the owning tool, stage a payload, and let the user (or Claude's description-matching delegation) trigger it. Each route maps one drift class to its owner. Keep this in sync with the verified plugin capability maps; do not invent owners.

routes.detects: CLAUDE.md names a command, path, or architecture that no longer matches the codebase (stale command, dead reference, outdated structure).
routes.action: Stale content in CLAUDE.md. The tool that rewrites it against the code is claude-md-improver — invoke it and point it at the stale items below.

routes.detects: A SKILL.md body references a scripts/, references/, or assets/ file that no longer resolves.
routes.action: A skill points at files that no longer exist. skill-creator owns skill repair — hand it the skill dir and the dangling references.

routes.detects: A skill's description frontmatter no longer matches what its body does (body added/removed capabilities the description doesn't mention).
routes.action: A skill's description has drifted from its body. skill-creator's description-optimization path re-aligns it for correct triggering.

routes.detects: A .claude/ component is malformed — invalid frontmatter, bad manifest, broken schema — independent of whether its content is current.
routes.action: A config file is structurally malformed (well-formedness, not staleness). plugin-validator checks the whole tree — point it at the plugin or .claude root.

routes.detects: A skill's description/trigger is weak or undertriggering (a quality concern, surfaced read-only).
routes.action: A skill's trigger may be too weak to fire reliably. skill-reviewer gives a read-only quality review — hand it the SKILL.md path.

routes.detects: A rule states a mechanically checkable prohibition that prose can't enforce (a deterministic predicate on a tool call), so it belongs in a hook, not a rule.
routes.action: This rule is really a deterministic predicate — hookify can codify it as a hot-reloadable enforcement rule. NOTE: hookify writes gitignored *.local.md, so it does NOT propagate to a team.

routes.detects: The codebase signals an automation that does not yet exist (formatter present but no PostToolUse hook; integration dep but no MCP; etc.). This is what is MISSING, not what is stale.
routes.action: The project would benefit from an automation it doesn't have. claude-automation-recommender proposes what's missing — Hestia maintains what exists; this is the seam.
